wiki.extract: The R. J. Reynolds Tobacco Company (RJR) is an American tobacco manufacturing company based in Winston-Salem, North Carolina. Founded by namesake R. J. Reynolds in 1875, it is the largest tobacco company in the United States. The company is a wholly owned subsidiary of Reynolds American, itself a wholly owned subsidiary of British American Tobacco.

topHazards.label: A small silicone valve in caps sold with the Podium and Peak Fitness water bottle can come loose and detach, posing a choking hazard.
The stroller's carrying handle can break and detach posing a fall hazard.

sampleRecalls.title: CamelBak Recalls Caps Sold with Podium and Peak Fitness Water Bottles Due to Choking Hazard
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves certain caps sold with CamelBak's Podium and Peak Fitness water bottles with three date codes. The date codes are H19039, H19063 and H19175 and are located on the underside of the cap. The water bottles were sold in a variety of colors in 17, 21- and 24-ounce sizes. CamelBak and Podium or Peak Fit…
hazards: A small silicone valve in caps sold with the Podium and Peak Fitness water bottle can come loose and detach, posing a choking hazard.
injuries: CamelBak has received 14 reports of the silicone valve coming loose in the cap and detaching. No injuries have been reported.
products: CamelBak Podium and Peak Fitness water bottles
units: About 46,000 (In addition, about 13,000 were sold in Canada)

sampleRecalls.title: Bugaboo Recalls Cameleon3 Strollers Due to Fall Hazard
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves the Bugaboo Cameleon3 strollers. The stroller has an aluminum and plastic frame with rubberized wheels, a removable seat and bassinet, a removable "U"-shaped carry handle, an under-the-seat storage bag and a sun canopy. The bassinet, seat and sun canopy come in a variety of colors. The removable c…
hazards: The stroller's carrying handle can break and detach posing a fall hazard.
injuries: Bugaboo has received 16 reports of carry handles breaking. No injuries have been reported.
units: About 9,200 in the U.S. and 960 in Canada

sampleRecalls.title: R.J. Reynolds Recalls Camel Metal Match Cigarette Lighters
sampleRecalls.description: September 22, 1994 Release # 94-138 Washington, D.C. - CPSC is working with R.J. Reynolds of Winston- Salem, N.C., which recalled yesterday about two million "Camel Metal Match" cigarette lighters that were distributed as part of a cigarette promotion. Use of the lighter may result in burn injuries. To date, R.J. Reyn…

sampleRecords.nature: Closed trauma involving internal organs, major blood vessels
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was loading snow guns and jacks onto a dump-style truck. The truck was parked on an incline and the door closed on the employee. The employee was hospitalized for contusions and a lacerated liver.

sampleRecords.event_description: Pedestrian struck by vehicle in roadway, unspecified
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was in the middle of a roundabout when they were struck by a vehicle, resulting in injuries to their head, neck, back, hips and legs.

sampleRecords.event_description: Injured by slipping or swinging object held by injured worker
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was chopping basil with a knife and severed the tip of his left thumb.

sampleRecords.event_description: Other fall to lower level 16 to 20 feet
sampleRecords.final_narrative: While descending a ladder from a roof, an employee slipped and fell 16 feet, requiring hospitalization.

sampleRecords.event_description: Struck against stationary object or equipment, n.e.c.
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was testing a slide ride at startup. The water was turned onto the slide, but had not yet filled the bottom pool when the employee tested it. The employee slid down the slide and, once at the bottom, slid across the shallow pool (1/4" of water) and into the end of the slide wall.  The employee fractured her right tibia and suffered ligament damage to both ankles.  The employee was hosp
